channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Kabam consolidating LA office and making layoffs
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 19, 2026

1 Min Read

Marvel Contest of Champions developer Kabam is consolidating its office in Los Angeles in a move that has resulted in an unknown number of layoffs.

A Kabam spokesperson confirmed the move to Game Developer but declined to comment on the job cuts. 

"Following a review of strategic priorities, Kabam made the decision to consolidate its LA office and streamline operations," reads the statement. "This wasn't a decision made lightly and the company remains focused on its slate and committed to its partners."

One person who spent over five years working at Kabam's office in LA indicated their role has been eliminated in a post on LinkedIn earlier this week.

Kabam first expanded into LA in 2015 by acquiring local studios Tapzen and Magic Pixel Games and merging them into a new Kabam-branded studio in Culver City. At the time, company leadership said the mobile specialist intended to strengthen its "deep" relationships with Hollywood partners.

Related:Report: Former Xbox president Sarah Bond and Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick linked to Peter Thiel's secret society

South Korean publisher Netmarble acquired Kabam's Vancouver studio and assets including Marvel Contest of Champions in 2016. Kabam's remaining studios were spun off into a new entity called Aftershock.

Netmarble US was rolled into Kabam in 2022, once again bringing an LA studio into the fold (thanks, PG.biz). 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/kabam-making-layoffs-after-choosing-to-consolidate-la-office
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Report: Former Xbox president Sarah Bond and Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick linked to Peter Thiel's secret society
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 19, 2026

2 Min Read

Background image by Olli Kilpi on Unsplash // Strauss Zelnick headshot via Take-Two // Sarah Bond headshot via Xbox

Former Xbox president Sarah Bond, former Roblox chief product officer Manuel Bronstein, and Take-Two president and CEO Strauss Zelnick have been linked with an invite-only society called 'Dialog' founded by German-American entrepreneur and conservative political activist Peter Thiel. 

Thiel is the controversial billionaire co-founder of fintech juggernaut PayPal and defence, AI, and software company Palantir. Notably, he was also one of Facebook's earliest backers and advised U.S. president Donald Trump when he was seeking election ahead of his first term (via The BBC). 

More recently, Thiel bankrolled republicans ahead of the 2026 mid-terms (thanks, Yahoo!) and held a series of off-the-record lectures about the antichrist—who he apparently believes might be walking among us in the guise of Greta Thunberg (via The Guardian). 

As reported by Wired, a deluge of internal records exposed by Swiss investigative journalist and hacktivist 'Maia Arson Crimew' have seemingly revealed the names of 113 people involved in Dialog. It's unclear whether those listed by Maia on Bluesky are society members, guests, or speakers. 

Related:Kabam consolidating LA office and making layoffs

The list includes some other notable figures from outside of the video game industry, including SpaceX and Tesla CEO Elon Musk, OpenAI chief strategy officer Jason Kwon, and (500) Days of Summer actor Joseph Gordon-Levitt—the latter of whom confirmed his attendance on Instagram but denied knowing Thiel personally.

As noted by Wired, Dialog has kept its rolodex of members under wraps for around two decades. The group convenes at off-the-record retreats to participate in ominously named sessions such as 'Navigating WWIII,' 'Build-a-Cult,' 'Battlefield Technologies,' and 'How's Your Sex Life?'. Additional documents and registration records obtained by Wired indicate Dialog also features talks more "typical" of a thought leadership conference. 

The publication added that Dialog attendees have included two U.S. senators, a former Middle East chief of intelligence, an ambassador to the United States, and multiple Google and Google DeepMind executives. 

According to Wired, the rise of artificial intelligence is seemingly weighing on the mind of Dialog attendees, with a sign-up form obtained by the publication (which asks those attending to predict the future) littered with responses indicating that AI will have a world-altering impact on work, war, and education. 

"Societal degeneration, will continue to accelerate." one person predicted, according to documents viewed by Wired. 

Related:Rockstar's legal setback, Final Fantasy tax incentives, and PlayStation swaps 'PC' for 'AI' - Patch Notes #57

Game Developer has reached out to Roblox and Microsoft for comment. Take-Two declined to comment when approached.

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/report-former-xbox-president-sarah-bond-and-take-two-ceo-strauss-zelnick-involved-in-peter-thiel-backed-secret-society
